entry.title: Incorporating Custom Events from Lobby into SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

What is SAP Build? 

  • SAP Build is a cloud-based tool for designing and prototyping applications with ease
  • User-friendly interface and drag-and-drop functionality make interactive prototypes without extensive coding
  • Supports real-time collaboration, seamless integration with other SAP solutions, and pre-built templates

In this blog, our goal is to explore the process of uploading event specifications to SAP Build Lobby and consuming those events in SBPA.

SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) enables business users to automate workflow processes and tasks without writing code.

Check here for What's New and Learn more from the roadmap.  

This August 2024, SAP Build Process Automation has a released a new way of discovering Business Events from the Lobby.

Check out this blog...

Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation: Manage Credit Memo Request 


Creating Event Projects from the Lobby: By Uploading Event Specification...

  • Navigate to the Connector Section in the Lobby:

First, you can access the Lobby section of SAP Build and navigate to the Connector area. This is where you will be able to manage and consume events within your application.

  • Events Have a Dedicated Place and Subpage:

Once in the Connector section, you will find that events have their dedicated place and subpage. This makes it easier for you to manage and work with different types of events for your application.

  • In this guide, we will focus on the Upload specification source when adding an event.
    As a user, you will see the create button on the Events subpage.

  • Events can be easily uploaded into the lobby of our platform.
    Presently, we support only JSON format for event specifications, with plans to expand to additional formats in the future.

For demonstration purposes, I have downloaded an example from BAH to illustrate this process. 

  1. Go to the Business Accelerator Hub page. 
  2. Select any of the products --> for this example -  SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  3. Select events on the tab below the Title and Short description.
    1. Select any event object that you wish --> for this example - Business Situation Events



  4. Download the file to your system. 

Any events in JSON format can be added, considering that the event mesh supports that specific event.

  • Event spec file uploaded will consists of Events and the Payload of Events:

    Each uploaded event object contains the event itself and its associated payload. This information is crucial for understanding the type and scope of each event that you are consuming.






  • Add the Event Object to the Build Lobby:

    Once you upload the desired event, you can easily view it in the Build Lobby.
    This will make the event accessible within your application.

 

  • Events can be Used Across Build Products:

    Currently, the Build Process Automation is utilizing uploaded events, with plans for Build Apps to also make use of uploaded events in the near future. This means that once you upload an event in the Lobby, it can potentially be utilized across different SAP Build products.

  • Discovering Events via Library in the Process Builder:

    After uploading an event in the Lobby, you can discover and utilize it through the library in the Process Builder. This ensures that the event is easily accessible for building and automating processes within your application.


 

Other use cases…

There are some additional steps to setup Event Mesh 
Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Tailored IT strategy planning: The key to successfully harmonized business and IT goals
entry.content:
In recent conversations with various IT leaders on their projects and challenges, answers on the purpose of further IT implementations are, as expected, considerably multilayered. They range from “simple” IT cost-saving goals (TCO reduction) and security aspects (cybersecurity) to the adaptation, or even the creation of entirely new business models.  

The following diagram shows a snapshot of the challenges that companies routinely consider, divided across business-driven and underlying IT topics:

Additional concerns also emerged from these discussions, including complexity, silos, and a lack of coordinated plans across the different areas. They revealed that stakeholders underestimated the interdependence and need for coherent planning. Subprojects often start before realizing the importance in having a comprehensive plan, and as a result, the projects become inconsistent.

When working on a single project, one goal may be attainable throughout the project. However, when the project is part of a larger program or initiative, its shortcomings become transparent and overall target achievement is far removed. In addition to project delays, the initially saved effort – whether in time or money – leads to frustration among stakeholders who fulfilled their goals from their project perspective.

While a siloed focus on local interests is challenging, so is selecting one or more partners for the transformation. This is where the shortcomings of a missing coherent planning basis become clear. Decoupled subproject requests for anything (RFXs) result in individual quotations that fall short of building an overarching, comprehensive transformation plan.

Unraveling project challenges

Thinking beyond ready-made solutions and services and standardized procedures and delivery objects can be challenging when addressing complex issues. For that reason, a business transformation advisor can be beneficial. The advisor enables SAP customers to determine whether tailored services are necessary and which methods and tools are needed early in the full-scope planning process.

Like a tailor creating a custom suit, a business transformation advisor defines a tailored consulting service by using the following process: 

  1. Collect mandate and define scope: A clear definition of the mandate and scope should be approved and supported by management during the project planning phase. This involves all elements of the business strategy, including the mission, target markets, current and future product and service offerings, and sales and people strategies. Topics, such as AI and sustainability, must be appropriately considered – after all, plans are being made for the next 5 to 10 years. This helps ensure a holistic IT strategy is defined and aligned with all company goals.
  2. Recognize and understand the strategic direction: The intent and path forward must be carefully considered. Key questions include who represents the company strategy and its drivers in this endeavor, what decision criteria can be derived from the strategic direction of the business, and how the mandate’s objectives match the plan’s objectives.
  3. Define decision criteria: All subsequent steps and deliverables must align with the company's goals. Which deliverables will be required to make strategic decisions? How does the decision-making process for products and projects work? Who is involved, and what are their respective priorities and requirements?
  4. Define the procedure model: Picking suitable methods and tools for developing defined delivery objects is critical. Different criteria play a role in the selection, such as who can adequately represent the business and provide information about current needs and future projects, where knowledge can be found, and which tools are needed to analyze process performance and systems. In addition, the effectiveness of the chosen project methodology must be assessed in light of business circumstances.
  5. Determine success factors: Implementing measures to achieve goals and ensure compliance becomes increasingly difficult throughout the project when specific factors are unidentified, undefined, or poorly communicated. Critical factors include active management sponsorship, sufficient time and budget, potential resistance, and appropriate countermeasures. Above all, involving the right and most important partners – such as the primary software supplier – is essential, with their accountability being key.

The outcome of this process – when clearly aligned and tailored to business goals – forms a stable digital IT foundation for the company’s strategy. This foundation, in turn, is the basis for implementing the corporate strategy, by which a customer’s CIOs, chief digital officers (CDOs), and their teams are ultimately measured.

The next step is selecting appropriate partners for the implementation. The RFx can only be carried out successfully if a clear and solid basis has been developed. Specifically, this refers to the necessary deliverables and general conditions mentioned earlier to evaluate and compare offers meaningfully.

Companies often do not clearly define the essential elements of a transformation in their RFPs. Typical examples include an unclear process scope, undefined migration approach or exclusions, and lacking integration of third-party products and add-ons. As a result, quotations cannot be adequately compared, because they differ in content, effort estimates and price and are unaligned with the transformation plan.

Mapping the way forward

When defining a corporate strategy, questions often arise about SAP's role as a software provider. Our customers want to know we have the necessary industry, process, and methodology knowledge and neutrality to provide competent advice.

Business transformation advisors at SAP are exceptionally skilled in answering all those questions using flexible methods and tools developed, based on experiences with various customer projects. For example, advisors leverage SAP Signavio solutions, such as SAP Signavio Process Insights, for the initial process analysis. Then, to evaluate the target architecture, they rely on SAP LeanIX solutions and the SAP Reference Business Architecture tool. This toolbox is based on the TOGAF Standard enterprise architecture methodology with different layers, starting with the business architecture.

It is important to compare the business capabilities derived from the strategy with the SAP solutions and the involvement of required expertise. Such instances will occasionally welcome a special SAP product or industry expert, or even a software developer, who is brought in, in addition, to solve critical challenges. The products that reach the target architecture remain the customer’s decision, supported by SAP’s desire to remain neutral.

Company situations, strategic goals, decision-making processes, and adjustments to the scope, methodology, and service are always possible and naturally move customers away from a standard service. After a more intensive exchange, in my experience, all decision-makers prefer a tailored approach.

Establishing confidence in business changes

At the end of the project, business transformation advisors help ensure the results presented to the management and project team correspond to all necessary decision criteria. Demonstrating the usefulness and necessity of the cooperation between service design and project execution gives involved parties the confidence that the right decisions have been made.

This phase usually comprises the following main deliverables:

  • IT target architecture derived from the business strategy with associated requirements, primarily business capabilities, process scope, IT solutions, and the IT architecture
  • Compatible transformation road map to create a step-by-step and logical sequence while taking into account the dependencies and capacities for realization, such as a suitable deployment model
  • Effort estimate of all resources – including roles, people, and internal and external cost estimations – as well as a proposed cost for software procurement and its operation
  • Cost-benefit analysis, depending on the conditions of the decision-making process (value proposition or business case including ROI, NPV calculation)

Delivering business changes that matter

IT organizations have been trending, for years, toward initiating preliminary efforts as business projects supported by the entire leadership team. Through the involvement and collaboration of corporate management, as well as business and IT representatives in the early phase, this approach helps ensure all interests are considered as agreed and key players are involved and prepared for subsequent projects.

The positive change management aspect of this approach should not be underestimated. Affected employees at all levels should be involved early, especially those from the business. This is a vital success factor in the overall transformation.

The “cloud mindset” and “new way of working” are just two examples that demonstrate the importance of the people-change aspect of transformation. With the increasing footprint of cloud solutions, it is necessary to understand and accept the associated standardization and limitations to individualization. The benefits of combining new processes with modern interfaces are also not to be overlooked – these changes are a prerequisite for younger generations of employees in their daily work.

 

Ready to harmonize your business and IT goals by tailoring your IT strategy with a plan for a solid technology foundation? Read the overview “Your Success Is Our Priority.”

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA委外采购 - 委外供应商MRP区域
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA对于委外采购业务，标准方案是为委外供应商创建委外类型（03）的MRP区域。

一些客户并没有为委外供应商创建MRP区域，在正确维护了特殊采购类型（如30）虽然可以正常的生成委外采购申请，但无论从需求供给的查看（如MD04或者Manage material coverage等应用），还是原材料发给供应商后的供应商库存，都会与工厂级别的需求、供给及可用库存混在一起。

当您为委外供应商创建MRP区域后，该委外供应商相关的需求供给或者供应商库存，都会从工厂级别独立开来。且无需在物料主数据上分配每个供应商的MRP区域.

当您使用Subcontracting Cockpit将原材料库存发给供应商后(Movement type 541)，库存数量从工厂MRP区域转移到了供应商MRP区域。

这样，您就能很清晰的掌握委外采购过程中的材料的需求及库存移动情况。

当然，采购申请能根据供应商独立开来到相应的供应商MRP区域，前提是采购申请上维护了委外供应商。如果您希望MRP运行出来的采购申请自动带出供应商，您需要为该物料创建此供应商的货源清单，并指定与MRP相关。

——————————————————————————————————————————
**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: 基于MTO产品的公司间销售退货流程介绍
entry.content:
总览

2408版本之前有个Note:3334657会提示，公司间退货流程2F4不支持E库存物料。

2408版本更新之后，此问题已经解决。客户可以使用基于MTO产品的公司间销售与公司间销售退货流程。

请先观看BLOG：基于MTO产品的公司间销售流程介绍 - SAP Community

流程介绍

1.参考2F4流程，创建公司间退货单，APP：Manage Customer Returns，参考公司间销售订单创建

行项目赔款方式，选择创建贷项凭证如下图：

注意：基于MTO产品的销售退货目前是不支持Replacement Product方式的，但是常规物料支持。

Save and Release退货单后，查看流程流中自动创建的退货交货单号和贷项凭证申请号

2.对上一步记录的退货交货单进行拣配和发货过账，APP：change outbound delivery

3.质检，APP：Enter Inspection Results - From Warehouse 

4.对第一步记录的贷项凭证申请创建贷项凭证，APP：Create Billing Documents

5.对第一步记录的退货交货单创建内部贷项凭证，凭证类型IG，APP：Create Billing Documents

检查IDOC是否创建成功：

6.查看供应商贷项凭证财务日记账，APP：Manage Journal Entries

前提配置

基于正向公司间销售配置完成后，需要对OPD增加一条配置：

entry.title: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Diving into Your Own System
entry.content:
Introduction

From 2402 Release, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition added a new system monitoring capability, collectively called the Technical Monitoring Cockpit for monitoring the ABAP system.  The Cockpit provides a seamless connection between your system workload and single ABAP statistics records (main records and sub-records) down to SQL statements and their prepared plan.  Now you have a good starting point to conduct performance analysis on the SAP S/4HANA system in case of high response times, especially when you are an administrator or a developer. 

As this Technical Monitoring Cockpit is not unique to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and the Business Technology Platform, the discussion here applies to them as well.

I plan to tackle this topic in a series of blogs in bite sizes so that you can easily consume them during your day-to-day work.

  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Clicking Through: this blog provides a guided tour of the Cockpit to readers, walkthrough (clicking through) various functions and resolve some confusions.
  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – The Key Concepts: this blog explains what metrics we are looking for in the Cockpit, and how they can be helpful to you, especially when something seems suspicious. 
  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Diving into Your Own System Workload:  this blog discusses the approach as an administrator/developer to analyze the system performance daily.
  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Analyzing Performance with A Customized Profile (to be published):  this blog discusses how to take the advantage of setting up a profile to record system data you are interested, hence analyze the data when issues surface.

In my previous two blogs of this Technical Monitoring Cockpit series, I explained how to click through these apps and the key concepts, now let’s put them into use in your own system: how to do a system analysis as an administrator/developer daily.

 

System Workload

When talking about the system monitoring, the first entry is the System Workload app (see my previous blog on how to access it).

The below screen System Workload: Response Time Contribution gives a glimpse on how the system is utilized in the past 24 hours (Note: my most business users are in Asia, so the heavy workload appears in my midnight to early morning since I am located in US East Coast.)

  • During the past 24 hours (12 pm on Oct. 9 to 12 pm on Oct. 10), heavy workload happened around 2 am to 6 am.
  • The peak Response Time Contribution reached 360 seconds.
  • If I am curious on the peak Response Time Contribution, I can move the Time Slider to left. Comparing with two graphs of Oct. 8 vs. 9, we found
    • The peak Response Time Contribution is about the same, 300+ seconds, although they occurred at a different time slot.
    • When focusing on the highest Response Time Contribution, the pattern is similar with three high components: the Roll Wait Time, the ABAP Processing Time and the HANA Execution Time.

The Peak Response Time Contribution from 12 pm to 12 pm on Oct. 9

 

The Peak Response Time Contribution from 12 pm to 12 pm on Oct. 8

 

Now let’s narrow the time range to 2:30 – 3:15 am. I want to find out the reason of high Roll Wait Time, ABAP Processing Time and HANA Execution Time.  By clicking on Roll Wait Time in the Legend, we can see the Remote Function Call (RFC) is the main contributor, almost 93% ((56.36+56.24+49.39+8.99+8.97+8.27)/201.76). The top three programs are QDEST_RUN, QDEST_RUN_DESTINATION and ARFC_RUN_NOWAIT.

Explore the Cause of Long Roll Wait Time

 

The Roll Wait Time represents a time for which the roll context was rolled out. In the below figure, it is a major contributor to an RFC Process or waiting time until the results are returned from an external system.  This matches with the nature of an RFC call, i.e., you have no control over an external system on how fast it returns the result to you.

Contributors to the Server Response Time in a Synchronous RFC Processing (from SAP Help)

 

Now I click on the ABAP Processing Time in the Legend, the top ten contributors are listed in the table.  The top one is to run a program called /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources in an OData V2 type request.

Explore the Cause of Long ABAP Processing Time

 

Go to the details (right most > button of the record), we can see this program was run seven times as listed in the table.  The processing time is quite even, between 1.46 to 2.04 seconds.

ABAP Processing Time of Running Program /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources (At 10-Minute Intervals)

 

In the blog Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Key Concepts, I explained how the ABAP Statistics Data are collected: one minute at a time on those completed work processes (look back), and data are aggregated. How about we look at the data in a different granularity?  For example, select As Collected? That is a different view (see below).  However, we still cannot pin down which vertical purple bar belongs to which of these seven runs.

ABAP Processing Time of Running Program /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources (As Collected)

 

To get the details of each run, I click on the Details button at the right of the top row. This brings me to the ABAP Statistics Record screen with more information about this record.

In the tab Server Response Time Contribution, it provides all the metric data of this program. The large portion of the time is spent on the ABAP Processing and HANA Execution.

ABAP Statistics Record – Server Response Time Contribution

 

When I see the Database Network Time is 148.54 milliseconds, it tells me there are many trips to the database.  I want to explore further by clicking on the tab Data Connections. There are 26,326 database calls.  This confirms a lot of trips were made to the database.

ABAP Statistics Record – Database Connections

 

By clicking on the Details button again, we can see the Database Connection Details screen.  Although there are 26,326 logical database requests, only 333 physical database requests happened. From database point of view, it is efficient as most data are cached.

Database Connection Details

 

You can also explore the SQL statements associated with this long ABAP Processing Time by clicking on the tab SQL Statements.  When clicking on the first row of the program, you can see the details of this SQL statement.

SQL Statement Details

 

Sampled Work Process Data

In the blog Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Key Concepts, I discussed there are two perspectives of exploring system performance.  One is the System Workload, which has data at every minute on those completed work processes.  Another one is called Sampled Work Process Data.  It is looking at data at every second including those currently running work processes. I use this perspective to identify the running programs after I notice a peak resource consumption from System Workload.  Let me explain that.

The below figure displays the Sampled Work Process Data at the same time range as above, from 12 pm on Oct. 8 to 12 pm on Oct. 9.

Sampled Work Process Data (from 12 pm on Oct. 8 to 12 pm on Oct. 9)

 

It is tricky to isolate these colorful bars around 3 am.  If you try to narrow down to 2:30 – 3:15 am, you don’t see any data.  So, you have to enlarge the time range, for example 18:15 – 8:37. After applying filter of OData V2, I got the below chart and table.

Sampled Work Process Data: ABAP CPU Time (18:15 – 8:37)

 

In the previous section, I was investigating a high ABAP CPU Time with the following information:

  • Request Entry Type: OData V2
  • Request Entry Name: /SAP/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/0001
  • Program Context: /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources
  • Start Time: 2024-10-09 02:34:57.92458200

 

Now I click on the first row with the same -  Request Entry Name: /SAP/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/0001.  A new screen opens with a time series of this Program Context but different Program Name. When I focus on the time range of 2:34:57.92458200, there are three programs running in that vicinity, namely:

  • /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP
  • CL_ACMRT_KERNEL_PROXY=========CP
  • /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP

Request Entry Point: OData V2 and /SAP/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/0001

 

Take the last program /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP as an example. I click on the Details button on the right. It reveals the Single Work Process Sample screen. I can review the Response Time and other information.

Single Work Process Sample – Work Process Metrics

 

When clicking the “ABAP Statistics Record” button, the ABAP Statistics for this Request Entry Point becomes available. Please keep in mind that this Record is an aggregated information of this Request Entry Point in the past minute, no longer at that second. But it is still good enough for you to isolate the program you are interested in.

ABAP Statistics Record for Program /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP

 

You can do the same for other two programs: /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP and CL_ACMRT_KERNEL_PROXY=========CP.

 

Conclusion

This blog discusses a daily routine work for an administrator or a developer on how to discover a high resource consumption activity, and find out the program causes that by using both the System Workload and Sampled Work Process apps.

 

References

  • SAP Help: Monitoring the System Workload
  • Blog: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Clicking Through
  • Blog: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – The Key Concepts 
  • Blog: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Diving into Your Own System Workload
  • Blog: Analyzing performance degradations in the ABAP environment in the Cloud

entry.title: Changeable Sales orders for Solution Orders
entry.content:
When you create a solution order with sales items and release them, the system automatically creates a sales order. Previously, these were display-only sales orders of type SOR, and you couldn't change them. Now, the system behavior has changed. When you release the sales item, changeable sales orders of type OR (standard orders) are created by default.

As a solution order specialist, you previously faced limitations when trying to edit and fulfill sales orders created from solution orders. Editing these sales orders was not possible directly. However, this process has now improved. You now have the flexibility to modify sales items created from solution orders directly, simplifying the process and saving time.

When you release a sales item in a solution order, the system forwards all its data to create a changeable sales order. Any additional changes must be made in the sales orders. You can navigate to the follow-up sales order to make necessary changes.  Rejection code, schedule lines, delivery & billing status, and plant are updated back to solution orders. The changes in the follow-up sales orders are indicated in solution orders.

The changes are also displayed in the change log, which is available in the sales item detail view. You can view the sync status in the change log to determine whether the changed field is synced to the solution order or not.

For more information on changeable sales order, you can refer to the video Changeable Sales Orders for Solution Orders.

Note:

  • The sales item created from solution orders can’t be deleted.
  •  You can’t change the product ID.
  •  Incomplete sales orders can’t be created from solution orders. 

 Now let’s talk about some examples where you can use changeable sales orders.

Example 1: Handling Freight Cost

Let’s assume a customer ordered packaging solution. During the fulfilment of the sales order, the freight cost is determined. As sales orders created from solution orders are editable now, the internal sales representative updates the price in the sales order to reflect the additional freight costs and generated the order confirmation. The solution order specialist noticed that the sales order item had been updated in the solution orders to include these added freight costs.

Example 2: Navigating Stock Issues with Third-Party Shipping

Imagine a scenario where a solution order specialist released a solution order for fulfillment, but a delay occurred because a part of the solution is out of stock. To mitigate the delay, the sales order representative converted the item category to accommodate the purchase process, which initiated a direct shipment to the customer from supplier. The solution order specialist received the updated notification in solution orders.

Example 3: Ensuring On-Time Delivery with Adjusted Shipping Conditions

Let’s assume for another solution, the solution order specialist scheduled the order using the standard shipping conditions based on the customer master data.  However, the customer later requested an urgent delivery. The internal sales update the shipping condition to speed up the delivery process. The solution order specialist could view this change on both the item list and the item details page and use the information to adjust the installation service schedule accordingly.

Example 4:  Applying Discounts after the Quote is Accepted

After the quote was accepted and the solution order was processed for fulfilment, the customer requested a special first-time discount upon reviewing the order confirmation. The solution order specialist confirmed the sales part had been sent for fulfilment but not billed. To prevent premature billing, a billing block was set, the discount manually applied, and the updated order confirmation verified. The billing block was then released to proceed with billing based on the revised pricing, which altered the net value of the sales items.

Activating Changeable Sales Orders

  • If you already use scope item Solution Order (4GT), note that the creation of display-only sales orders of type SOR is deprecated and will be deactivated with an upcoming release. Raise a ticket to the application component: CRM-S4-SOL-SLO to activate the creation of changeable sales orders of type OR (standard orders).
  • If you haven’t used the scope item Solution Order (4GT) before and you activate it now, the system creates changeable sales orders of type OR (standard orders) by default.

entry.title: How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP
entry.content:
Terry Penner and Jürgen Butsmann discuss the latest developments in SAP Business AI for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).  The conversation focuses on how Artificial Intelligence is evolving at SAP, with particular attention to large language models (LLMs) and their growing application in SAP cloud ERP and SAP BTP.

Listen to the podcast: Spotify | Apple Podcasts

Topics discussed:

Part 1: Evolution of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

  • Latest developments in the AI market
  • SAP’s ERP strategy evolution for AI
  • Latest solution architecture updates

Part 2:  Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

  • What is Joule
  • Differentiating AI offerings
  • How can AI add value to a business
  • Partner role with SAP Business AI

Part 3: Cloud ERP AI Priorities

  • SAP cloud ERP AI development priorities
  • SAP’s Business AI scenario priorities

This blog is a follow-up to AI for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: deep-dive discussion

About the speakers:

Jürgen Butsmann is part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Marketing team responsible for AI in the context of SAP S/4HANA, finance and digital supply chain. Jürgen has been with SAP for more than 26 years.  He is a recognized expert for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP .

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP marketing team for Business Applications, focused on SAP BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 23 years of technical and business experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Part 1: Evolution of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Latest developments in the AI market

Terry: In today's discussion, we'll explore how Artificial Intelligence for SAP S/4HANA and the SAP Business Technology Platform is evolving. Jürgen, what new developments have occurred in the AI market since we last spoke?

Jürgen: The evolution of technology continues unabated. We're observing an increase in services such as document services and advanced large language models (LLMs). The LLMs we see today are notably more fact-based, not just designed for entertainment purposes like the early iterations of ChatGPT, but for fact-oriented applications like those seen with Aleph Alpha. Additionally, there have been multiple iterations and diverse models emerging tailored to specific needs. We've also noticed a decline in pricing, which makes these AI services increasingly attractive and accessible in the market.

Terry: Absolutely. We've also witnessed significant legal developments, with content owners like The New York Times and others looking to withdraw their material from large language models, underscoring the importance of having robust and compliant data sources for these models.

SAP’s ERP strategy evolution for AI

Terry: How has SAP's ERP strategy for AI evolved?

Jürgen: Our strategy has evolved significantly, placing a more substantial emphasis on an AI-first approach across SAP. This shift means a lot of new ideas and pre-made implementations for our customers. We've taken a two-pronged approach in our investments. First, we're enhancing end-user productivity by integrating digital assistants into our systems, with Joule being a primary component. Although still early in deployment, these tools signify a shift from traditional graphical user interfaces to more intuitive digital assistant interactions.

Further confirming this direction, our CEO, Christian Klein, anticipates that in ten years, most system users will interact with digital assistants rather than traditional interfaces. Our focus also extends to natural language interactions within these systems, fostering intelligent activities and responses.

 

Secondly, we are concentrating on enhancing end-to-end processes. While we continue to enhance user interaction and productivity on the front end, significant efforts are directed towards optimizing large-scale processes, particularly within enterprise service management. This holistic approach ensures not just improvements in individual functionalities but also the acceleration of end-to-end process flows, all underpinned by advanced AI and generative AI technologies. This dual focus aligns with our commitment to transforming business processes through innovative AI applications.

Latest solution architecture updates

Terry: What are the latest updates to SAP's AI solution architecture since then?

Jürgen: One major change is the ongoing development of Joule. Initially, Joule had a rather rigid architecture that was not very flexible and did not support LLMs. This has changed. We have introduced the capability to create prompts using LLM support, which is a priority that has helped increase acceptance of this digital system and co-pilot for everyday work. Additionally, we've improved context sensitivity—knowing where you are in your system and what steps you are processing. This is a key differentiator from other co-pilots on the market and allows us to integrate responses that feel less artificial and more contextually aware.  It's able to  integrate proprietary documents into the context of the business processes.

Furthermore, we continue to develop its extensibility. We've opened possibilities for customers to tailor the co-pilot to perform functions specific to their needs.

Terry: And regarding extensibility, that’s a significant area where SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) collaborates with the ERP team by supplying developer tools and experiences, such as the vector engine within SAP HANA, to support these functionalities—it's a joint effort.

Part 2:  Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

What is Joule?

Terry: We’ve spoken quite a bit about Joule. Could you clarify exactly what Joule is?

Jürgen: Essentially, Joule serves as an interface for natural language commands within our system architecture. It acts as an entry point where users can interact with the system to trigger functions or retrieve necessary business process information. While Joule leverages AI technologies, its use doesn’t alter the inherent capabilities of our AI. It fits seamlessly into our system, making those use cases distinct and noteworthy. Ideally, Joule simplifies user interactions by providing a natural, conversational interface with the system. While our systems could operate without Joule, using it enhances the user experience, making interactions more engaging and straightforward.

  

Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

Terry: What's the difference with SAP Business AI? How should customers differentiate potential AI offerings?

Jürgen: The top priority for SAP Business AI is to provide clear and measurable value to customers. Before using AI scenarios, there must be a reasonable and measurable return on investment. The focus is on implementing AI in SAP systems without the need to change generic functionality, where the system's context details can be used to address processes more effectively.

The reliability of AI use cases is a major focus, based on fact-based foundation models whenever possible. All AI-assisted processes undergo an ethical check and are supported by the data security of SAP Business AI architecture.

We are also continuing to refine our pricing approach and evaluating if we will continue using current AI units or shift to different technologies in the future. Customers consider usage, potential value add, costs, and investments when determining the break-even point for their use cases. Moreover, customers expect our AI solutions to be user-friendly.

Terry: Adding to that, not all AI use cases deliver sufficient value for application. It's crucial to concentrate on scenarios with high-quality underlying data linked to large language models. For example, one effective use of AI I've observed involves searching through support cases and providing recommendations to new team members, enhancing the search experience and improving how information is interpreted and suggested.

How can AI add value to a business

Terry: How would you explain to a customer how AI can add value to a business?

Jürgen: When considering how AI supports business processes, the user's connection to the system is paramount. Ideally, this happens through a digital co-pilot, like Joule in our case. Users interact with Joule by posing questions that prompt queries to a large language model, which in turn provides responses. However, large language models may lack specific business process details since those are unique and not universally predefined, leading to potentially incomplete answers. 

 

To address this, we might extend our architecture to incorporate calls to the ERP system or access other proprietary data, ensuring no sensitive business information is exposed to potentially vulnerable cloud environments. We balance accessibility with security by using a vector database to locate and integrate the right business-specific data from outside the large language model's scope.

As users submit human language requests, these are processed through the digital system and converted into technical prompts by a large language model. These prompts trigger the vector engine to retrieve the necessary information. The returning data can then be refined further by the large language models, which are all deployed securely within our system environment to prevent data leakage.

The data retrieval process benefits from enhancements such as semantic similarities and pattern recognition to continuously improve response accuracy. Once processed, the information is delivered back to the user, completing the cycle and delivering the value we aim for with our AI-enabled systems.

This systematic approach ensures that AI not only supports but also enhances business processes effectively while maintaining data integrity and security.

Terry: So, we could describe Joule as the front-end experience, distinct from the backend LLM that powers the AI functionalities.

Partner role with SAP Business AI

Terry: What role do partners play in this space?

Jürgen: Firstly, our partners have a deep understanding of our customers. It's crucial that partners are well-versed in our setup, strategy, architecture, pricing, and other elements that shape how we envision utilizing these services. We leverage our partners to create scalable solutions that precisely address specific customer needs. They are instrumental in implementing individual projects that tackle unique business challenges at the customer sites. Our partners have a proven track record of handling such use cases effectively.

Part 3: AI Extensibility and SAP BTP

SAP cloud ERP AI development priorities

Terry: What are some of the other AI development priorities at SAP cloud ERP, and how is SAP BTP supporting those AI scenarios?

 

Jürgen: We're integrating various technologies beyond what we've already discussed, like our work with live language models. For example, merging our capabilities with Microsoft Copilot offers expanded possibilities for enhancing natural language interactions. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and the AI Foundation are pivotal in anchoring these flexibilities. This integration allows us to broaden our scope and reinforce the support we provide for cloud ERP AI scenarios.

SAP’s Business AI scenario priorities

Terry: What business scenarios are central to SAP's Business AI focus?

Jürgen: Our focus isn't narrow, but we do see significant interest particularly in the finance sector. Currently, we're engaging extensively with businesses, customers, and partners to pinpoint key priorities specific to finance. Our approach begins by assessing what immediate actions we can take and then contemplating more visionary possibilities for the future. For example, one idea is the automatic creation of forecast scenarios based on historical data within our systems. This capability could enhance management, strategy, and decision-making meetings significantly, even without all finance experts gathered at one table. Additionally, there are numerous other aspects of finance where AI can play a transformative role.

Terry: We will link to some specific use cases within the podcast notes. Thank you, Jürgen, for joining us and sharing your insights into AI for cloud ERP and the business technology platform. It's been enlightening.

Jürgen: Thank you for having me. I hope our discussion helps people understand better where we are now and where we are headed with our technology.

[ Note:  This blog was adapted from the podcast transcript by an SAP system using Azure OpenAI–gpt-4 ]

Key links:

  • Four ways to infuse business-ready, trusted AI into your processes
  • SAP BTP for S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Business AI

Read more in this series:

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: Planning to move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud? The CQC Transformation Assessment will help you succeed
entry.content:
Are you planning a move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud? Excited about the possibilities and eager to make the right decisions early on? Looking to identify areas where your team's knowledge needs to be strengthened? Finding the right tools, services and learning resources from SAP can be time-consuming. This is where the continuous quality check (CQC) for transformation assessment can make a real difference.

“Continuous quality checks” are SAP Enterprise Support services that are available with every cloud subscription.

The CQC Transformation Assessment will evaluate your project team’s understanding of SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics, project challenges, and essential tools. It will help keep your project on track throughout the SAP Activate phases and provide personalized recommendations, including best practices and enablement resources.

 

An Example of Planning CQC Services for an SAP S/4HANA Private Edition Project*

(*Please note that not all available services are included)

 

An Example of Planning CQC Services for an SAP S/4HANA Public Edition Project*

(*Please note that not all available services are included)


Key Benefits:

  • Avoid known and potential pitfalls early in your project phases.
  • Save time and effort by keeping your project team updated on the latest SAP tools and accelerators.
  • Learn from past projects to prevent known and potential pitfalls.
  • Receive personalized SAP best practice recommendations as needed.

🎥Watch our short video for an expert view on why you should avail of this service.

Check out the info sheets for detailed information on when and how to request it, and visit SAP Enterprise Support value maps for further guidance.


Get Started with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Find the right content to accelerate the value of your solution with SAP S/4HANA Cloud value maps from SAP Enterprise Support. Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can navigate to curated content, be directed to self-service platforms, engage with fellow customers and SAP experts, and discover continuous quality checks.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:   

  1. One-time sign-up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required (please allow 2 hours process time)
  2. Access value maps 
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for  SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Once in the value maps, we welcome you to collaborate in our forums, ask questions or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further help. Value maps are easy to navigate but an on-boarding call allows us to tailor the conversation to suit your needs and highlight specific content for you. 

Got a question about the value maps, or our content?
Leverage the “ask questions” feature in the value map or contact us:
sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

There is a new commenting feature available for What’s New. Authors can now evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Key Differences Between SAP Central Business Configuration and the Implementation Activities App
entry.content:
Are you struggling to understand when to use SAP Central Business Configuration and when to use the Implementation Activities app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-system landscape? No problem, let’s clear that up.

SAP Central Business Configuration is best used for configuring business processes based on your specific requirements. This involves performing configuration activities, accessing setup instructions or setup guides, and maintaining an overview of the statuses of these activities. You can also download a list of configuration activities for offline use and reference in the future.

To easily identify the right configuration activities, you can search by name or configuration activity ID. Furthermore, you can make use of the various filter options that enable you to filter by status, go-live relevance or business area. Moreover, the Country/Region filter allows you to filter configuration activities that are relevant to one or multiple countries/regions in scope or globally. This should come in handy especially for scope extensions that involve new countries/regions.

Screenshot of the Configure Your Business Processes app in SAP Central Business Configuration

The Implementation Activities app is more suited for displaying and maintaining current settings in the development, test, and production systems. It allows you to compare configuration activities among these systems and view change logs for the configuration activities.

Screenshot of the Implementation Activities app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

To learn more, you can go to the SAP Help Portal for further details about configuration activities in SAP Central Business Configuration and the Implementation Activities app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. To stay in the know, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition bundle
entry.content:
GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition

Since March 2023, customers who have selected SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core ERP system to run their businesses have received additional entitlements beyond the core functionality. These added capabilities form one of two comprehensive bundles: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium.
 
In this blog, I will focus on the base edition bundle, providing a clear explanation of each entitlement and its business value. I will discuss the premium edition in a separate blog, which I will hyperlink here once created.

This blog is designed for business users and consultants with ERP experience, aiming to provide practical insights into each entitlement's purpose and when it’s most valuable. Please note, statements about adoption difficulty and required personas are based on my research and experience, and other cases may vary based on the specific business context etc.
Note 1: Hyperlinks to SAP4ME will take you directly to the relevant page if you're logged into a productive SAP4ME system and have suitable rights
Note 2: This blog speaks to the entitlements included as of 2024-10-17

Blog Outline:

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Base: Overview
  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
  4. Base Entitlement: Finance - Group Reporting
  5. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Process Automation
  6. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition
  7. Base Entitlement: BTP - CPEA Credits
  8. Base Entitlement: SAP Digital Access
  9. Base Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition
  10. Closing Remarks

 

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Base: Overview
    The Base Edition comes in two SKUs: 8014771 for global markets and 8014782 for the Greater China region (which excludes some entitlements). This blog focuses on SKU 8014771.

    How to identify if you have this SKU:
    1. Log in to SAP for Me: Reporting > Purchased Solutions
    2. Under 'Orders by Solution Area,' select S/4HANA Public - your SKU will be listed on the right. If 8014771 appears with a valid date, you have the GROW Base Edition. If 8014761 appears, you have Premium. Other SKUs likely represent SKUs that do not include this full suite of entitlements.

  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    The core entitlement includes:
    1. Services & Support: Access to support services like incident creation, onboarding advisor support, and the SAP Enterprise Support Portfolio, which offers learning content and services such as Continuous Quality Checks.
    2. Systems: Access to multiple systems (e.g., ERP production, test, and development systems) and related tenants. This also includes non-ERP systems like Cloud ALM, CBC, SAP for Me, and BTP systems.
    3. Functionality: Access to pre-configured scope items. Commonly used ones include J59 - Accounts Receivable and J60 - Accounts Payable. You can view and manage scope items through SAP Activate’s Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items accelerator.
    4. Full User Equivalent (FUE): Your contract includes a specific number of FUEs that govern how many business users can access core functionality. Check your licensed FUE quantity in SAP for Me: Reporting - License Consumption under metric CY334.
    5. Entitlement Measurement: Overuse is possible but monitored by SAP. Your account team will reach out in case of overconsumption.

  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
    What is an "entitlement"?
    An entitlement refers to the right to use a specific set of functionalities that may normally require extra payment or licenses. In the GROW Base Edition, certain features are included up to a threshold without extra software licensing costs. For example, Group Reporting is included up to 25 objects in the base edition.

    Are the "entitlements" completely free?
    The outlined entitlements mean there's no software licensing cost if you use up to the amount indicated. Usage beyond that quantity will require additional (paid) licenses, and you may need to consider some investment of business time and effort to adopt new features and functions, and in some cases you'll require consultants for the setup and implementation.

    What does "adoption" mean in this blog?
    I'm using 'adoption' to refer to the setup AND usage a software functionality. The setup or implementation is the process of adopting, and once it's setup and being actively used  - then I would then say the customer has adopted a feature/entitlement.
    Base Edition - Additional Entitlements:
    GROW with SAP Public Edition, base - additional entitlements
  4. Base Edition Entitlement: Group Reporting
    Business Case
    Group Reporting helps businesses that operate multiple subsidiaries or legal entities shorten the time needed for financial consolidation. It automates key activities and streamlines the data model, making it highly valuable for businesses with complex financial structures. For single-entity businesses, this functionality is not immediately relevant.

    Business Role Relevance
    Senior finance roles, such as Finance Managers and Group Controllers, will benefit from this functionality for managing financial consolidations.

    Entitlement
    The GROW Base Edition includes 25 Group Reporting objects. An object typically refers to a legal entity within a consolidation group (e.g., parent and subsidiary companies). Additional licenses are required for more than 25 objects. Official documentation is here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption using metric CH112 in SAP for Me under SAP4ME: Reporting -> License Consumption.

    Overusage
    It is possible to over-consume your allocated Group Reporting objects. SAP will periodically review your usage under metric CH112. You can license additional units by contacting SAP for SKU 8013710.

    Scope Item Relevance
    The main scope item for Group Reporting is 1SG. This measures whether data from group reporting has been posted into the system. You can validate its usage in Portfolio & Products - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Usage. Other Scope Items flagged as Group Reporting relevant are  287, 28B, 4R7, 2U6

    More Information
    For more detailed guidance on adopting Group Reporting, refer to this blog post. This deeper descriptions, webinar links, and crucially it outlines the key steps to adopting group reporting

    Key Adoption Notes:
    Expertise needed: You'll need a combination of BTP administrators and finance consultants for configuration and initial setup.
    Adoption Effort:  Rated 3 (on a scale from 1 to 5). You’ll need finance consultants, and technical consultants with expertise in BTP to set up this functionality.
    Not to be confused with: This entitlement does not include Group Reporting Data Collection, which must be licensed separately.

  5. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Process Automation
    Business Case
    SAP Build Process Automation enables non-developers, such as key users or business process experts, to create bots or automated workflows based on a set of rules. This is valuable when no standard ERP functionality exists to meet a specific business need, for example, automating the creation of general ledger accounts.

    Business Role Relevance
    Consultants, process automation specialists, and business key users involved in implementing and running the ERP system will find this useful.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes:
    20 Standard Users (CP278) – Can trigger or interact with a process.
    2 Advanced Users (CP279) – Can design, monitor, and administrate processes.
    1 Unattended Automation (CP283) – A bot that continuously monitors and executes a process.
    Documentation of these entitlements are here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP278, CP279, and CP283 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License

    Overusage
    You are blocked from overconsuming these licenses. Once the entitlement limit is reached, additional licenses must be purchased or allocated through CPEA credits.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no specific scope item for SAP Build Process Automation.

    More Information
    This community page has overview videos and links to all other resources. This area of the API hub is where you find SAP pre-created templates.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: You’ll need a BTP expert for the base setup. Then you'll need LoB experts e.g. finance experts to develop meaningful automations as per your needs. Setup steps are outlined here.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for platform setup by BTP experts; 2-4/5 for workflow creation depending on complexity.

  6. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition

    Business Case
    SAP Build Workzone allows organizations to create a central homepage for employees with user-specific access to applications across various SAP systems. This enhances efficiency by providing direct links to necessary business apps for roles like sales managers or purchasing clerks. The more SAP systems used throughout your organization, the more value this solution will offer your organization.

    Business Role Relevance
    This functionality benefits employees across departments who frequently use SAP applications in their daily tasks.

    Entitlement
    You receive 1 Workzone user per FUE licensed. For example, if you have licensed 100 FUEs for the core, 100 users can access SAP Build Workzone.

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP264 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption

    Overusage
    Additional licenses or CPEA credits are required if the number of users exceeds your entitlement.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no scope item related to SAP Build Workzone usage.

    More Information
    More documentation is on the SAP Build Workzone Discovery Center.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: A BTP global administrator for initial setup, followed by non-technical key users for managing pages.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for BTP experts; 3/5 for non-experts. View the setup steps in this tutorial.
    Not to be confused with: SAP Build Workzone is distinct from SAP Start, which a more lightweight solution without ability to consider other SAP system authorizations. SAP Build Workzone Advanced Edition is a different product without a migration path from the standard edition.

  7. Base Edition Entitlement: CPEA Credits

    Business Case
    CPEA Credits (Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) allow businesses to consume various BTP services within predefined limits without additional fees. These credits can be applied to services like Integration Suite, SAP Build Apps, and Cloud Identity Services. You can view the list of included services here in the Discovery Center. There's more nuance to the CPEA credit commercial model, which you can read about here.

    Business Role Relevance
    Technical teams managing BTP services will oversee consumption of this entitlement. The business areas that consume this depend on which services you're utilizing from BTP - many are in the technical / administration area.

    Entitlement
    Customers receive 1% of their net Annual Contract Value in CPEA credits, with a minimum value of €2,000 and a maximum value of €16,000.

    License Consumption
    You can view your CPEA credit balance in SAP4ME - Finance and Legal - Enterprise Agreements

    Scope Item Relevance
    CPEA credit usage is not tied to any scope item, as it applies across various BTP services.

    More Information
    Each service that can be paid for by CPEA credits are listed here in the Discovery Center - and this same resource provides additional information on each service.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP experts and business area owners are required to manage and utilize CPEA credits effectively.
    Adoption Effort: Varies depending on the BTP service being used.
    Not to be confused with: CPEA credits differ from BTPEA and EA credits, which operate similarly but cover different services.

  8. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Digital Access

    Business Case
    SAP Digital Access governs how non-human interactions (such as external systems creating documents) are licensed. It is relevant when integrating third-party systems like Salesforce to trigger document creation within SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

    Business Role Relevance
    IT and integration specialists responsible for system integrations with non-SAP platforms will need to manage this entitlement.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes 100,000 digital access documents per year.

    License Consumption
    Track your consumption by launching the License Compliance Digital Access app in the productive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This guidance document shows how to do this and provides other useful context on measuring your digital access usage for SAP S/4HANA

    Overusage
    It is possible to exceed your annual document allowance. SAP periodically reviews your consumption and the account team will reach out in the case of overconsumption.

    Scope Item Relevance
    This entitlement applies to external system interactions and is not tied to specific scope items.

    More Information
    Refer to the Digital Access documentation for a more detailed explanation.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: No specific competency is needed. Entitlement consumption should be reviewed by the party responsible for your SAP system billing & commercials.
    Adoption Effort: 1. There's nothing to setup per se for Digital Access - you only be aware of it and monitor your consumption.
    Other Notes: SAP Digital Access is only necessary for non-SAP systems. If SAP systems trigger document creation, digital access is not consumed.

  9. Base Edition Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition

    Business Case
    Ariba Buying Base Edition allows employees to make internal purchase requisitions. It's scope is similar to the core ERP requisitioning scope of 18J, but Ariba Buying Base has an enhanced UX and has the capability to integrate with many advanced features, such as directly connecting to supplier catalogs and the Ariba Business network.

    Business Role Relevance
    All company employees can benefit from an improved user experience for purchase requisitions, particularly in organizations using self-service requisitioning.

    Entitlement
    There is no access limit for the base functionality, but additional licensing may be required for extended capabilities, such as linking to external supplier catalogs.

    License Consumption
    There are no limits to usage of this entitlement. It operates more like a standard scope item, but many of the functionalities that bring Ariba Buying Base to life will require additional licenses.

    Scope Item Relevance
    Scope item 4AI measures whether you've setup the communications arrangement required to use Ariba Buying base edition.

    More Information
    View an overview of Ariba Buying in this video demo - but note this video includes the utilization of catalogs that may require additional licensing.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP administrators and procurement experts are required for setup and catalog integration.
    Adoption Effort: Unclear
    Other Notes: A lot of the functionality’s value comes from adding catalogs and integrating with the broader Ariba Network, which may require additional licensing.

  10. Closing
    The intent of this blog was to give you a base understanding of the each entitlement in the base edition - what it is, and when it's most valuable.
    Any feedback regarding on the content including other use cases, other resources to link etc. are welcome and I'll periodically update this blog with improved information.
